description: Starting a goroutine that blocks forever sending on a channel nobody reads after the consumer returns early.
body: Failure mode: a goroutine does `ch <- result` but the receiver returned (timeout, error) without draining, so the sender blocks permanently and the goroutine, its stack, and captured memory leak. Detection signals: goroutine count grows over time in pprof /debug/pprof/goroutine; memory creeps up under load; a deadline path abandons workers. Do not send on an unbuffered channel without a guaranteed receiver or cancellation. Safer fallback: use a buffered channel sized to the senders, select on ctx.Done() in the send, or use errgroup with a derived context, then verify goroutine count is stable across N requests.
